# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:  # Define your job's stage/environment information in GitLab CI (or other tool you are using) here; it is a comma-separated list of strings where each string represents an environment variable for the pipeline to use and define additional settings on that specific line. The variables defined will be available across all jobs within this or any following stages, depending upon which one runs first
stages:  # Define your job's stage/environment information in GitLab CI (or other tool you are using) here; it is a comma-separated list of strings where each string represents an environment variable for the pipeline to use and define additional settings on that specific line. The variables defined will be available across all jobs within this or any following stages, depending upon which one runs first
variables:  # GitLab Variable definition section; set your project's name here (mandatory) as well as other parameters like repository directory path where you have checked out source code from git. It is also possible to pass secrets and environment variables in this part of the file for CI/CD pipeline, which are not accessible via .gitlab-ci.yml script
  REPO_DIR: /home/ubuntu/repo    # define your repository directory path here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PROJECT_DIR' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for source code checkout from the given location in CI process. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing repo
  PROJ_NAME: ${PWD##*/}    # define your project name here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PROJECT' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current directory basename. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing proj
  RUNNING_ENV: prod    # define your running env here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PIPELINE_STAGE' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting the stage at which CI/CD pipeline is being run. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing Running Env
  PIPENV: ~/.local    # define your pipenv home directory here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PROJECT' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current project location. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing pi
  REPO_URL: https://github.com/username/repo    # define your repository url here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_REPOSITORY' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current repo location from CI process, and also to clone the code in specific commit
  PROJ_URL: https://github.com/username    # define your project url here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PROJECT' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current repository from CI process, and also to clone the code in specific commit
  GITLAB: https://gitlab.com    # define your github url here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_SERVER' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current server location from CI process, and also to access the project/repository
  GITLABTOKEN: ${VARIABLE}    # define your github token here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_JOB_TOKEN' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current job run in CI process. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing GITLAB TOKEN
  SONATYPEUSERNAME: ${VARIABLE}    # define your Sonatype username here; you can use GitLab variable 'SONATYPE_USER' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current user name in the repository, which is used to upload artifacts from Jenkins/Nexus
  SONATYPEPASSWORD: ${VARIABLE}    # define your Sonatype password here; you can use GitLab variable 'SONATYPE_PASS' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current user pass in the repository, which is used to upload artifacts from Jenkins/Nexus
  KEYSTOREID: ${VARIABLE}    # define your keystore id here; you can use GitLab variable 'KEY_NAME' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current key name in the repository, which is used to upload artifacts from Jenkins/Nexus
  KEYSTOREPASSWORD: ${VARIABLE}    # define your keystore password here; you can use GitLab variable 'KEY_PWD' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current key pwd in the repository, which is used to upload artifacts from Jenkins/Nexus
  EXTENDEDFILES: ~/.extendedfiles    # define your extended files directory here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PROJECT' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current project location. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing Extended Files
  REPO: my/repository    # define your repo path in Github here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_REPOSITORY' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current repository name from CI process. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing Repository
  PAGE: my/page    # define your page path in Github here; you can use GitLab variable 'CI_PIPELINE' or any other available gitlab environment variables like this (mandatory) as well for getting current pagename from CI process. If it is not defined then pipeline will fail due to missing Page
